nl_prompt: What was total gross revenue last quarter?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.line_value) AS total_gross_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: How many orders were placed this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is our average order value?
generated_sql:
SELECT AVG(line_value) AS average_order_value
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many unique customers made a purchase in the last 6 months?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS CustomerCount
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 6 MONTH)

nl_prompt: What is the total number of products in our catalog?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS ProductCount
FROM products

nl_prompt: What was our highest single-order revenue ever?
generated_sql:
SELECT MAX(line_value) AS max_revenue
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many orders were cancelled this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_order_count
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'cancelled'
  AND order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is our total revenue for Electronics products?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
WHERE products.category = 'Electronics'

nl_prompt: What is the revenue for the top-selling product?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.line_value) AS top_selling_product_revenue
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
GROUP BY products.name
ORDER BY SUM(orders.line_value) DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: How many new customers signed up this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customers.id) AS CustomerCount
FROM customers
WHERE created_at >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND created_at < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is our total cost of goods sold this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(products.cost_price * orders.quantity) AS COGS
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
WHERE order_date >= '2023-01-01' AND order_date <= '2023-12-31'

nl_prompt: What percentage of orders were returned?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) / COUNT(*) * 100 AS ReturnRate
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many distinct product categories do we sell?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT category) AS ProductCategoryCount
FROM products

nl_prompt: What was our total revenue last year?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is the revenue breakdown by product category?
generated_sql:
SELECT products.category, SUM(orders.line_value) AS revenue
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
GROUP BY products.category
ORDER BY revenue DESC

nl_prompt: Which customers placed more than 5 orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.name, COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
GROUP BY customers.name
HAVING COUNT(orders.id) > 5
ORDER BY order_count DESC

nl_prompt: Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime revenue?
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.name, SUM(orders.line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
GROUP BY customers.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: What is the average days between order and shipment per product category?
generated_sql:
SELECT products.category, AVG(logistics_deliveries.delivery_date - orders.order_date) AS avg_days_between_order_and_shipment
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
JOIN logistics_deliveries ON orders.shipping_id = logistics_deliveries.shipping_ref
GROUP BY products.category
ORDER BY avg_days_between_order_and_shipment DESC

nl_prompt: Which product categories have more than 10 percent return rate?
generated_sql:
SELECT products.category, COUNT(CASE WHEN returns.order_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) * 100 / COUNT(orders.id) AS return_rate
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
LEFT JOIN returns ON orders.id = returns.order_id
GROUP BY products.category
HAVING return_rate > 10
ORDER BY return_rate DESC

nl_prompt: What is the revenue per customer segment?
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.segment, SUM(orders.line_value) AS revenue_per_segment
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
GROUP BY customers.segment
ORDER BY revenue_per_segment DESC

nl_prompt: Which sales rep closed the most orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT sales_reps.rep_name, COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count
FROM orders
JOIN sales_reps ON orders.sales_rep_id = sales_reps.id
GROUP BY sales_reps.rep_name
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: What is the monthly revenue trend for the last 12 months?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.line_value) AS revenue, DATE_TRUNC('month', orders.order_date) AS month
FROM orders
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month

GROUP BY p.name
HAVING COUNT(o.id) = 0

nl_prompt: What is the average order value per customer segment?
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.segment, AVG(orders.line_value) AS average_order_value_per_segment
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id

nl_prompt: What is the revenue from completed orders versus pending orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN line_value ELSE 0 END) AS completed_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'pending' THEN line_value ELSE 0 END) AS pending_revenue
FROM 
    orders

nl_prompt: Which product has the highest total quantity sold?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold
FROM order_items oi
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY total_quantity_sold DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: What is the total discount given across all orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(line_value) AS total_discount
FROM orders

nl_prompt: What is the average number of items per order by category?
generated_sql:
SELECT products.category, AVG(order_items.quantity) AS average_items_per_order
FROM orders
JOIN order_items ON orders.id = order_items.order_id
JOIN products ON order_items.product_id = products.id
GROUP BY products.category
ORDER BY average_items_per_order DESC

nl_prompt: What percentage of revenue comes from our top 20 percent of customers?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN customers.id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders ORDER BY SUM(orders.line_value) DESC LIMIT 20)) / SUM(orders.line_value) * 100 AS top_20_percent_revenue
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id

nl_prompt: Which logistics carrier has the worst on-time rate for top revenue customers?
generated_sql:
SELECT l.carrier_name, AVG(CASE WHEN ld.status_code = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time_rate
FROM logistics_deliveries ld

JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
WHERE c.segment = 'Top Revenue'
GROUP BY l.carrier_name
ORDER BY on_time_rate DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: Summarize delivery complaint themes for top 10 revenue customers.
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.name, COUNT(logistics_deliveries.status_code) AS complaint_count
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
JOIN logistics_deliveries ON orders.shipping_id = logistics_deliveries.shipping_ref
WHERE orders.line_value > 0
GROUP BY customers.name
ORDER BY SUM(logistics_deliveries.status_code) DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: Which logistics partners have most damaged goods complaints?
generated_sql:
SELECT logistics_deliveries.carrier_name, COUNT(logistics_deliveries.status_code) AS complaint_count
FROM logistics_deliveries

GROUP BY logistics_deliveries.carrier_name
ORDER BY complaint_count DESC
LIMIT 5

nl_prompt: What do churned customers say were top reasons for leaving?
generated_sql:
SELECT customers.name, COUNT(returns.id) AS churn_count
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
LEFT JOIN returns ON orders.id = returns.order_id
WHERE orders.status = 'canceled'
GROUP BY customers.name
ORDER BY churn_count DESC
LIMIT 10
